figures.caption: Table 2: Classification accuracies evaluating spatial scaling across classical channels (C). The Trained qDTW reflects the optimal universal configuration (L = 3, Triplet Loss, β = 0.01).

Table 5: Classification accuracies using the Local Observable (⟨Z0⟩) architecture compared against the Adjoint PreEmbedding Ansatz. Evaluated wit L = 3, Triplet Loss, and β = 0.01. Results from reference Classical DTW and the proposed Pre-Embedding Ansatz are taken from Table 2.

Figure 1: Comparison of classical distance metrics on phase-shifted sequential data. (Left) Rigid Euclidean distance strictly evaluates identical temporal indices, completely failing to match the structural peaks of the signals. (Right) Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) allows one-to-many index matchings, successfully aligning the corresponding structural features regardless of temporal distortion.

Figure 5: Multivariate Adjoint Re-uploading Circuit configured for C = 3. The classical data encoding block S(x) (comprising parallel Rx rotations) and trainable entanglement block V (θ) are repeatedly interleaved L times. The inverse sequence yj structurally mirrors the forward sequence xi, measuring the final geometric overlap on the ground state.

Figure 3: The Unified Pre-Embedding Adjoint Circuit computing dQ(xi, yj) for C = 3. The trainable Ansatz V (θ) prepares a high-dimensional basis state prior to classical data encoding. Sequence xi is loaded forward, while sequence yj and the entanglement block are mathematically inverted. Local distance is extracted via a global geometric fidelity measurement onto the ground state |00 . . . 0⟩.

Figure 7: Ablation study evaluating circuit depth (L ∈ {1, 3, 5}) across four multivariate time-series datasets. The primary y-axis (solid lines) displays peak classification accuracy, while the secondary y-axis (dashed lines, log scale) indicates the final gradient variance. The data demonstrates that L = 3 provides the optimal balance between spatial expressivity and optimization stability, whereas L = 5 triggers a barren plateau and representation collapse in the majority of datasets.
